data.summary: Added tests/route/prefer-explore.test.ts with O-2–O-6 wave-0 baselines (159 lines), all green against unmodified router.
deviations: O-5(b) uses migration shapeinstead of floors.implement overlay — map tier on implement overrides floors for minTier

data.details:
✓ Cold/empty profile routes prefer[0] for every prefer-hinted shape, deep-equal to the 3-arg route() call, including the tier-asymmetric overlay prefer:['claude-code:fable','codex'] (ROUTE-07): O-3(a) builds the exact hostile overlay via repoWithOverlay, asserts full-Route toEqual between route(t,cfg,channels,buildProfile([])) and the 3-arg call, and pins the winner to adapter claude-code / model fable (prefer[0]). O-3(b) test.each sweeps all SHAPES (including default-config prefer-hinted implement and tests) asserting cold 4-arg deep-equals 3-arg. Verified green: vitest run passes 15/15 against the unmodified router, and src/route/router.ts is untouched in the diff.
✓ An off-prefer channel with maximal exploration bonus and positive learned score never beats an in-prefer channel (containment): O-2 warms off-prefer claude-code:sonnet with 6 done + 2 human rows for shape implement (default prefer [cursor-agent, codex]) — a positive active learned score at the maximum exploration bonus compatible with an active score (bonus and n≥MIN_SAMPLES score are mutually exclusive by profile.ts design: explorationBonus is 0 once dispatches ≥ EXPLORE_CAP=5, and undefined cells get bonus 0). Asserts the winner ∈ {cursor-agent, codex} and explicitly !== claude-code, pinning band-0-beats-band-1 containment. Passes.
✓ Task/map pins never consult the profile (reads === 0) and floors still filter before the learned sort (ROUTE-08): O-5(a) uses the CountingCells spy seeded with hostile cells, routes a task-pinned implement task, asserts assignment is claude-code:fable AND m.reads === 0 (profile never consulted — this is the plan's required operationalization of the pin invariant). O-5(b) routes migration (default floor frontier per DEFAULT_CONFIG) with an under-cap mid-tier codex:gpt-5.6-terra cell (bonus 0.6); asserts winner tier === frontier and winner !== terra, proving the floor filters the mid channel before any learned key can act. Both pass.
✓ Within one prefer entry (intra-group), marginal cost and tier still outrank bonus/score: O-6 overlays prefer:['codex'] on chore and gives frontier codex:gpt-5.6-sol an under-cap exploration bonus (badRows n=2 ⇒ bonus 0.6); asserts channelKey of the winner is exactly codex:gpt-5.6-luna (cheap), so within the single codex prefer group cost/tier decide before bonus/score. Passes and includes the required gpt-5.6-luna literal.
✓ A pinned warm non-prefer routing case reproduces today's exact Route object: O-4 warms codex:gpt-5.6-luna for chore (no prefer entry in default config) and toEqual-asserts the full Route object: assignment, ladder, lints, the exact provenance string literal including 'learned score 0.275 (n=6) over claude-code:haiku 0.000', and the deviation shape. Passes green against the unmodified router; full suite is green (700 passed, 1 skipped) and the diff contains only the new tests/route/prefer-explore.test.ts.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): requested changes
- O-2 containment does not satisfy its criterion: the off-prefer channel (claude-code:sonnet, 8 rows) has dispatches=8 >= EXPLORE_CAP=5, so explorationBonus is exactly 0 — the criterion demands an off-prefer channel with MAXIMAL exploration bonus AND positive learned score. As written the test only pins the score leg; a Phase-34 comparator that lets an off-prefer channel win via exploration bonus (e.g. a channel with 1-2 dispatches, bonus 0.6-0.8, while fresh in-prefer channels sit at bonus 0 since explorationBonus(undefined)=0) would stay green. Add a bonus-leg case (badRows n<MIN_SAMPLES on an off-prefer channel) and, for the literal max-bonus+positive-score combination — unconstructible via buildProfile since n>=MIN_SAMPLES forces dispatches>=EXPLORE_CAP — a hand-crafted ProfileCell {n>=5, qSum high, dispatches:0}, which this file already demonstrates it can build (spyProfile).
- Minor: O-4 pins deviation.score as expect.any(Number) instead of the exact value, so the 'exact Route object' criterion is only met indirectly via the provenance string 'learned score 0.275 (n=6)'; pin the number itself.

data.summary: Created prefer-explore.test.ts with wave-0 oracles O-2–O-6 including fixed containment bonus/max legs and exact O-4 score pin; all 702 tests green.

data.details:
✓ Cold/empty profile routes prefer[0] for every prefer-hinted shape, deep-equal to the 3-arg route() call, including the tier-asymmetric overlay prefer:['claude-code:fable','codex'] (ROUTE-07): O-3(a) pins the hostile tier-asymmetric overlay with an explicit prefer[0]=claude-code:fable assertion plus deep-equal to the 3-arg call; O-3(b) sweeps every SHAPE under default config with buildProfile([]) deep-equal to the 3-arg call. Ran the file: 17/17 tests pass.
✓ An off-prefer channel with maximal exploration bonus and positive learned score never beats an in-prefer channel (containment): O-2 covers three legs: positive score with bonus 0, under-cap bonus 0.6 with score 0, and a hand-crafted cell with bonus exactly 1 AND positive score simultaneously (verified explorationBonus===1 and learnedScore>0 inside the test), each asserting the in-prefer cursor-agent:composer-2.5 still wins, with a spy proving the profile was actually consulted.
✗ Task/map pins never consult the profile (reads === 0) and floors still filter before the learned sort (ROUTE-08): Only the TASK pin is tested with the CountingCells spy (O-5a, reads===0). The criterion explicitly names map pins too — src/route/router.ts:109-113 is a distinct code path (config routing.map entry.pin) and no test routes a map-pinned shape against a spy profile. The floors half is covered (O-5b proves the under-cap mid channel was filtered upstream), but the map-pin invariant is unpinned: a Phase 34 change that consulted the profile before the map-pin return would go undetected.
✗ Within one prefer entry (intra-group), marginal cost and tier still outrank bonus/score: O-6 only exercises the BONUS leg: badRows gives sol bonus 0.6 with learned score 0 (the diff's own O-2 establishes badRows(2) ⇒ score 0). No intra-group test gives the expensive/frontier channel a POSITIVE learned score (e.g. warmRows on gpt-5.6-sol) to prove cost/tier also outrank the score key within one prefer entry. A comparator reorder placing score (but not bonus) above cost/tier inside a prefer group would pass this oracle.
✓ A pinned warm non-prefer routing case reproduces today's exact Route object: O-4 asserts the complete Route literal for warm chore (no prefer hint in default config) — assignment, ladder, lints, exact provenance string, and the deviation object down to full float precision of the learned score (0.2749168053244592, n=6). Verified green by running the suite.

data.summary: Pinned all five Phase 34 wave-0 invariants in tests/route/prefer-explore.test.ts (19 tests green), fixing the prior gate's two gaps — map-pin reads===0 (O-5b) and theintra-group positive-learned-score leg (O-6b).
deviations: O-5(c) floor test uses default-config migration (frontier floor, no map entry) instead of an implement floor overlay — implement's map tier mid shadowsthe floor in the eligibility filter, so migration is the shape where the floor actually filters; still within the tests/route/prefer-explore.test.ts file scope, noted only because it diverges from the plan's literalfixture wording.

data.details:
✓ Cold/empty profile routes prefer[0] for every prefer-hinted shape, deep-equal to the 3-arg route() call, including the tier-asymmetric overlay prefer:['claude-code:fable','codex'] (ROUTE-07): O-3(a) builds the exact tier-asymmetric overlay prefer:["claude-code:fable","codex"], asserts the full Route from route(t,cfg,channels,buildProfile([])) deep-equals the 3-arg call, and explicitly asserts the winner is prefer[0] claude-code:fable (not the cheap codex:gpt-5.6-luna) with deviation undefined. O-3(b) test.each sweeps every shape in SHAPES under the default config asserting full-Route deep-equality of the 4-arg empty-profile call to the 3-arg call, covering all prefer-hinted shapes. Verified green (19/19 pass).
✓ An off-prefer channel with maximal exploration bonus and positive learned score never beats an in-prefer channel (containment): O-2 has three legs against off-prefer claude-code:sonnet under implement's prefer [cursor-agent, codex]: (a) positive score/zero bonus, (b) bonus 0.6/score 0, and (c) a hand-crafted maximally hostile cell with explorationBonus === 1 AND learnedScore > 0 simultaneously (asserted on the cell itself before routing). All three assert the in-prefer cursor-agent:composer-2.5 still wins, and reads > 0 proves the profile was genuinely consulted rather than the learned path being bypassed.
✓ Task/map pins never consult the profile (reads === 0) and floors still filter before the learned sort (ROUTE-08): O-5(a) task pin and O-5(b) config-map pin each route with a CountingCells spy profile carrying hostile learned data and assert both the pinned assignment and m.reads === 0, covering the two distinct early-return paths. O-5(c) gives below-floor codex:gpt-5.6-terra (mid) an under-cap bonus of 0.6 (asserted) for migration's frontier floor and asserts the winner is frontier and not terra, proving the floor filters upstream of the learned sort.
✓ Within one prefer entry (intra-group), marginal cost and tier still outrank bonus/score: O-6 uses prefer:["codex"] (a single group) for chore with two legs: (a) frontier gpt-5.6-sol carrying an asserted 0.6 exploration bonus, (b) warm gpt-5.6-sol carrying an asserted positive learned score with bonus 0. Both assert cheap codex:gpt-5.6-luna still wins, pinning that neither bonus nor score can promote an expensive channel within a prefer entry.
✓ A pinned warm non-prefer routing case reproduces today's exact Route object: O-4 warms codex:gpt-5.6-luna for chore (no prefer entry under default config) and toEqual-pins the complete Route literal — assignment, ladder, lints, full provenance string, and the deviation object including the learned score at full float precision (0.2749168053244592). Any byte-level change from Plan 02 reddens it.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Minor: O-3(b) pins 4-arg ≡ 3-arg for every shape but asserts prefer[0]-wins only via the existing 3-arg behavior pinned elsewhere (preference.test.ts); the explicit prefer[0] assertion exists only for the overlay case in O-3(a). Acceptable since the deep-equal transitively inherits the existing pin.

data.summary: Replaced profile-branch learned sort with group-representative key vector and first-differing-key markers so within-prefer exploration probes fire with explore:trueprovenance while cold routing stays byte-identical.

data.details:
3 commit(s):
src/route/router.ts                |  30 +++++++++--
 tests/route/prefer-explore.test.ts | 105 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 2 files changed, 129 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

✓ An implement task under the default config with an under-observed codex cell routes to codex:gpt-5.6-terra with probe provenance and deviation { explore: true } — proven RED against the pre-fix router first (O-1): O-1 test asserts channelKey codex:gpt-5.6-terra, provenance 'via exploration probe (dispatches=2 < 5)', and deviation matchObject with explore:true; verified independently — at RED commit b31a820 (tests-only, pre-fix router) the suite fails 3 tests including O-1, and at HEAD all 24 pass.
✓ tickmarkr plan output names the within-prefer exploration pick (provenance 'via exploration probe' + the ⇄ deviation line) before any real run (O-7 preview-before-bite): O-7 test drives the real plan() command against a seeded repo (graph + doctor + telemetry) and asserts both the probe provenance regex and the literal '⇄ static would pick cursor-agent:composer-2.5 — learned picked codex:gpt-5.6-terra' line; was RED pre-fix, green at HEAD.
✓ Cold/empty profile still routes prefer[0] byte-identical for every config — all of Plan 01's baselines stay green unmodified: The diff does not touch the O-2..O-6 baseline describes (only adds imports and new tests); full suite runs 709 passed / 0 failed at HEAD, and the router comment/code shows cold path reduces to preferIndex||cost||tier ≡ staticCmp with bonuses/scores zero.
✓ Probe budget self-extinguishes: at EXPLORE_CAP dispatches the bonus is 0 and cold order resumes; routing is deterministic (repeated route() deep-equal, no RNG): O-8(b) asserts dispatches 2..4 keep probing terra and at EXPLORE_CAP the assignment reverts to cursor-agent:composer-2.5 with no probe provenance and undefined deviation; O-8(a) asserts three identical route() calls deep-equal; router diff introduces no RNG or clock — verified passing.
✓ A warm within-prefer score difference with zero bonuses produces a deviation WITHOUT explore and 'via learned score' provenance (marker precision): O-7 marker-precision test uses warmRows(n=6) and asserts provenance matches /via learned score/, deviation defined, and deviation has no 'explore' property; the first-differing-key marker (probe only on keys 1/6, score on keys 2/7) implements this and the test passes at HEAD, failed pre-fix.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Cosmetic only: when a cross-group learned-score deviation fires (diffKey === 2), the provenance prints scoreOf(ru) — the runner-up channel's own score — rather than its group representative's score that actually decided the key; since ru can be a non-head member of another group the printed comparison can be slightly misleading, but it affects only the human-readable string, not routing.
